chief_complaint: A 78-year-old male presented with an 11-hour history of chest tightness.
history: Acute occlusive myocardial infarction (OMI) complicated by ventricular pre-excitation. The patient had a reduction in the extent of localized ventricular pre-excitation accompanied by secondary ST-T segment abnormalities in leads V1-V3.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Emergency coronary angiography revealed occlusion of the left anterior descending artery.
correct_diagnosis: Acute occlusive myocardial infarction of the left anterior descending artery
differentials: Acute occlusive myocardial infarction of the left anterior descending artery
Myocardial infarction due to right coronary artery occlusion
Acute myocardial infarction due to occlusion of the circumflex artery
correct_ranking: Acute occlusive myocardial infarction of the left anterior descending artery
Myocardial infarction due to right coronary artery occlusion
Acute myocardial infarction due to occlusion of the circumflex artery
